1_thailand_applicability.rationale: Thai government extension and a documented farmer case in Nakhon Pathom converge on the conclusion that fingerroot is cultivated successfully across Thai regions, with KUBOTA corroborating commercial-scale viability.

2_climate.rationale: Useful Tropical Plants supplies the quantitative envelope (12-35C, 1200-3000mm, up to 1000m, pH 6-7), Kew POWO confirms native habitat as wet tropical lowland, and DOAE recommends May-June planting; three independent sources converge.

3_soil.rationale: DOAE specifies 25-30cm tillage and 200-300 kg/rai lime, KUBOTA confirms raised-bed dimensions, and Useful Tropical Plants supplies the international pH range — multi-source convergence on sandy loam and acid-soil correction.

4_water.rationale: Three Thai sources (DOAE, KUBOTA, OPSMOAC) independently report watering every 2-3 days and stress that fingerroot tolerates moisture but not standing water; the Nakhon Pathom farmer case validates this in flood-prone Central Thailand.

5_planting.rationale: DOAE provides bud count, fungicide soak duration, and 400 kg/rai seed rate; OPSMOAC documents farmer-level rhizome conditioning (2-3 week pre-storage, 20cm spacing); KUBOTA confirms commercial vs home-garden spacing — all three converge on May-June planting.

6_care.rationale: DOAE specifies ammonium sulfate 50 kg/rai at sprouting and 200 g/hole chicken manure rooting; KUBOTA adds the 30 kg/rai balanced fertilizer at 3 months. Marked medium because fertilizer programs vary by region and individual soil tests are advised.

7_pests_disease.rationale: Both Thai sources identify rhizome rot as the dominant disease and prescribe pH/lime/drainage/crop-rotation prevention; specific insect pest detail is generalized from the Zingiberaceae context, hence medium rather than high.

8_harvest.rationale: DOAE distinguishes 5-6 month basic vs 8-9 month optimal-compound harvest; KUBOTA reports 7-8 months commercial; OPSMOAC documents the farmer harvesting at 7 months with Nov-Apr window. Multi-source agreement on ranges and yellowing-foliage cue.

9_economics.rationale: OPSMOAC supplies the only Thai farmer-level economics (15,000-20,000 baht/rai cost; 200,000-300,000 baht/rai income) but it is a single-farmer COVID-era anecdote — explicitly framed as such with caveats; UTP yield 10-30 t/ha provides international corroboration of the 2-3 t/rai range. Confidence kept at medium due to single-farmer income source and market volatility.

10_thailand_specific.rationale: OPSMOAC Phang-Nga and OPSMOAC Chachoengsao supply Thai cultural/regional context including the rakkluai cultivar and yellow-vs-black disambiguation; KUBOTA documents the COVID-19-era market expansion. Three Thailand-context sources support the regional breakdown.

11_foreign_knowledge.rationale: Frontiers 2025 supplies the comprehensive bioactive and pharmacology review with explicit incomplete-toxicology caveat; UBU Pharmacy and TU ATTM converge on traditional decoction 5-10g and 0.08% essential oil; JTTAM provides quantitative crude-drug quality benchmarks; UTP supplies international cultivation envelope. Five sources, three high-confidence.

overall_rationale: Cultivation, harvest, and Thailand-context sections all rest on at least 2-3 independently converging high-confidence Thai government or university sources, with international corroboration from Kew POWO and Useful Tropical Plants. Pharmacology section relies on Frontiers 2025 review and Thai pharmacy/medical-school references, all peer-reviewed or university-curated. Economics is the only section held to medium because the income figures derive from a single-farmer case study; this is explicitly disclosed in the body.
